description: Using `extcodesize(addr) > 0` to detect whether an address is a contract fails during construction. When code runs inside a constructor, `extcodesize(address(this))` returns 0 because the contract's code is not yet deployed. An attacker can deploy a contract whose constructor calls the victim—the victim sees `extcodesize(caller) == 0` and treats the caller as an EOA, bypassing contract-specific restrictions.
name: Bypassable Contract Existence Check via extcodesize
remediation: - Do not rely on `extcodesize` to distinguish EOAs from contracts. - If contract-only or EOA-only logic is required, use a different mechanism (e.g., trusted registry, explicit opt-in) or accept that constructor calls cannot be distinguished.
